frameworks.name: interval timing
frameworks.one_line: Estimating a target sub-second to seconds-range time interval via motor response

frameworks.name: pupil-linked arousal
frameworks.one_line: Pupil diameter indexes task- and feedback-related arousal responses

frameworks.name: trial-by-trial feedback adjustment
frameworks.one_line: Adaptive updating of response times based on previous-trial feedback

frameworks.name: dopaminergic modulation of motor control
frameworks.one_line: Levodopa replacement in GCH1-deficient dopamine biosynthesis

core_question: How do GCH1 mutations and levodopa treatment affect time estimation, trial-to-trial adjustment, and arousal responses in dopa-responsive dystonia?
key_assumptions: Pupil dilation indexes task- and feedback-related arousal
Time estimation accuracy reflects intact internal timing mechanisms
Trial-to-trial response time adjustment indexes feedback-driven adaptation
ON vs OFF levodopa contrast isolates dopaminergic contribution
manipulations: DRD patients vs matched healthy controls
Trial-by-trial feedback on a 1200 ms target interval
key_findings: Time estimation accuracy comparable to controls ON medication but reduced OFF medication
Task-related pupil responses showed the reverse pattern (larger OFF or smaller ON)
Trial-to-trial response time adjustments were reduced in DRD, particularly OFF medication
interpretations: Dopamine modulates timing performance and feedback-driven trial-to-trial adjustment
Pupil-linked arousal compensates or shifts inversely to performance under dopamine depletion
